heading: United States policy regarding the Freely Associated States
label: 1988 United States policy regarding the Freely Associated States

children.label: A
children.text: the Federated States of Micronesia, during such time as it is a party to the Compact of Free Association set forth in section 201 of the Compact of Free Association Act of 1985 (; note);Public Law 99–23948 U.S.C. 1901

children.label: B
children.text: the Republic of the Marshall Islands, during such time as it is a party to the Compact of Free Association set forth in section 201 of the Compact of Free Association Act of 1985 (; note); andPublic Law 99–23948 U.S.C. 1901

children.label: C
children.text: the Republic of Palau, during such time as it is a party to the Compact of Free Association between the United States and the Government of Palau set forth in section 201 of Joint Resolution entitled “Joint Resolution to approve the ‘Compact of Free Association’ between the United States and the Government of Palau, and for other purposes” (; note).Public Law 99–65848 U.S.C. 1931

children.heading: Agreements to furnish care and services
children.label: A Agreements to furnish care and services
aa
children.text: licensure, certification, registration, and tort issues relating to health care personnel;

children.label: bb
children.text: the scope of health services the Secretary may furnish, as well as the means for furnishing such services; and

children.label: cc
children.text: matters relating to delivery of pharmaceutical products and medical surgical products, including delivery of such products through the Consolidated Mail Outpatient Pharmacy of the Department of Veterans Affairs, to the Freely Associated States;

children.label: II
children.text: clarify the authority of the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to pay for tort claims as set forth under subparagraph (C); and

children.label: III
children.text: clarify authority and responsibility on any other matters determined relevant by the Secretary of Veterans Affairs or the governments of the Freely Associated States.

children.heading: Scope of agreements
children.label: ii Scope of agreements
children.text: The agreements described in clause (i) shall incorporate, to the extent practicable, the applicable laws of the Freely Associated States and define the care and services that can be legally provided by the Secretary of Veterans Affairs in the Freely Associated States.

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: Not later than 90 days after entering into an agreement described in clause (i), the Secretary of Veterans Affairs shall submit the agreement to the appropriate committees of Congress.

children.heading: Appropriate committees of Congress defined
children.label: II Appropriate committees of Congress defined
aa
children.text: the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources, the Committee on Foreign Relations, and the Committee on Veterans’ Affairs of the Senate; and

children.label: bb
children.text: the Committee on Natural Resources, the Committee on Foreign Affairs, and the Committee on Veterans’ Affairs of the House of Representatives.

children.heading: Payment of claims
children.label: C Payment of claims
children.text: The Secretary of Veterans Affairs may pay tort claims, in the manner authorized in the first paragraph of , when such claims arise in the Freely Associated States in connection with furnishing hospital care or medical services or providing medical consultation or medical advice to a veteran under the laws administered by the Secretary, including through a remote or telehealth program.section 2672 of title 28

children.label: A
children.text: conduct robust outreach to, and engage with, each government of the Freely Associated States;

children.label: B
children.text: assess options for the delivery of care through the use of authorities provided pursuant to the amendments made by this subsection; and

children.label: C
children.text: increase staffing as necessary to conduct outreach under subparagraph (A).

children.label: A
children.text: continue to make available to the Federated States of Micronesia, the Republic of the Marshall Islands, and the Republic of Palau, grants for services to individuals eligible for such services under part B of the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act ( et seq.) to the extent that those services continue to be available to individuals in the United States;20 U.S.C. 1411

children.label: B
children.text: continue to make available to the Federated States of Micronesia and the Republic of the Marshall Islands and make available to the Republic of Palau, competitive grants under the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 ( et seq.), the Carl D. Perkins Career and Technical Education Act of 2006 ( et seq.), and part D of the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act ( et seq.), to the extent that those grants continue to be available to State and local governments in the United States;20 U.S.C. 630120 U.S.C. 230120 U.S.C. 1450

children.label: C
children.text: continue to make grants available to the Republic of Palau under part A of title I of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 ( et seq.), the Adult Education and Family Literacy Act ( et seq.), and the Carl D. Perkins Career and Technical Education Act of 2006 ( et seq.);20 U.S.C. 631129 U.S.C. 327120 U.S.C. 2301

children.label: i
children.text: subpart 1 of part A of title IV of the Higher Education Act of 1965 ( et seq.);20 U.S.C. 1070a

children.label: ii
children.text: subpart 3 of part A of title IV of the Higher Education Act of 1965 ( et seq.); and20 U.S.C. 1070b

children.label: iii
children.text: part C of title IV of the Higher Education Act of 1965 ( et seq.);20 U.S.C. 1087–51

children.label: E
children.text: require, as a condition of eligibility for a public institution of higher education in any State (as defined in section 103 of the Higher Education Act of 1965 ()) that is not a Freely Associated State to participate in or receive funds under any program under title IV of such Act ( et seq.), that the institution charge students who are citizens of the Federated States of Micronesia, the Republic of the Marshall Islands, or the Republic of Palau tuition for attendance at a rate that is not greater than the rate charged for residents of the State in which such public institution of higher education is located; and20 U.S.C. 100320 U.S.C. 1070

children.label: F
children.text: continue to make available, to eligible institutions of higher education, secondary schools, and nonprofit organizations in the Federated States of Micronesia, the Republic of the Marshall Islands, and the Republic of Palau, competitive grants under the Higher Education Act of 1965 ( et seq.).20 U.S.C. 1001

children.heading: Other formula grants
children.label: 2 Other formula grants
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (1), the Secretary of Education shall not make a grant under any formula grant program administered by the Department of Education to the Federated States of Micronesia, the Republic of the Marshall Islands, or the Republic of Palau.

children.label: A
children.text: the Head Start Act ( et seq.);42 U.S.C. 9831

children.label: B
children.text: subpart 3 of part A of title IV of the Higher Education Act of 1965 ( et seq.); or20 U.S.C. 1070b

children.label: C
children.text: part C of title IV of the Higher Education Act of 1965 ( et seq.).20 U.S.C. 1087–51

children.heading: Authorization of Department of Defense programs
children.label: c Authorization of Department of Defense programs

children.heading: Department of defense medical facilities
children.label: 1 Department of defense medical facilities
children.text: The Secretary of Defense shall make available, on a space available and reimbursable basis, the medical facilities of the Department of Defense for use by citizens of the Federated States of Micronesia, the Republic of the Marshall Islands, and the Republic of Palau, who are properly referred to the facilities by government authorities responsible for provision of medical services in the Federated States of Micronesia, the Republic of the Marshall Islands, the Republic of Palau, and the affected jurisdictions (as defined in ).section 1921c(e)(2) of this title

children.heading: Participation by secondary schools in the Armed Services Vocational Aptitude Battery Student Testing Program
children.label: 2 Participation by secondary schools in the Armed Services Vocational Aptitude Battery Student Testing Program
children.text: It is the sense of Congress that the Department of Defense may extend the Armed Services Vocational Aptitude Battery (ASVAB) Student Testing Program and the ASVAB Career Exploration Program to selected secondary schools in the Federated States of Micronesia, the Republic of the Marshall Islands, and the Republic of Palau to the extent such programs are available to Department of Defense dependent secondary schools established under and located outside the United States.section 2164 of title 10

children.heading: Judicial training
children.label: d Judicial training
children.text: In addition to amounts provided under section 261(a)(4) of the 2023 Amended U.S.-FSM Compact and the 2023 Amended U.S.-RMI Compact and under subsections (a) and (b) of Article 1 of the 2023 U.S.-Palau Compact Review Agreement, for each of fiscal years 2024 through 2043, the Secretary of the Interior shall use the amounts made available to the Secretary of the Interior under to train judges and officials of the judiciary in the Federated States of Micronesia, the Republic of the Marshall Islands, and the Republic of Palau, in cooperation with the Pacific Islands Committee of the judicial council of the ninth judicial circuit of the United States.section 1990(c) of this title

children.heading: Eligibility for the Republic of Palau
children.label: e Eligibility for the Republic of Palau

children.heading: National Health Service Corps
children.label: 1 National Health Service Corps
children.text: The Secretary of Health and Human Services shall make the services of the National Health Service Corps available to the residents of the Federated States of Micronesia, the Republic of the Marshall Islands, and the Republic of Palau to the same extent, and for the same duration, as services are authorized to be provided to persons residing in any other areas within or outside the United States.

children.heading: Additional programs and services
children.label: 2 Additional programs and services
children.text: The Republic of Palau shall be eligible for the programs and services made available to the Federated States of Micronesia and the Republic of the Marshall Islands under .section 1921g(a) of this title

children.heading: Programs and services of certain agencies
children.label: 3 Programs and services of certain agencies
A
children.text: The Legal Services Corporation.

children.label: B
children.text: The Public Health Service.

children.label: C
children.text: The Rural Housing Service.

children.heading: Consultation with International Financial Institutions
children.label: g Consultation with International Financial Institutions
children.text: The Secretary of the Treasury, in coordination with the Secretary of the Interior and the Secretary of State, shall consult with appropriate officials of the Asian Development Bank and relevant international financial institutions (as defined in ), as appropriate, with respect to overall economic conditions in, and the activities of other providers of assistance to, the Freely Associated States.section 262r(c) of this title

children.heading: Establishment of a unit for the Freely Associated States in the Bureau of East Asian and Pacific Affairs of the Department of State and increasing personnel focused on Oceania
children.label: i Establishment of a unit for the Freely Associated States in the Bureau of East Asian and Pacific Affairs of the Department of State and increasing personnel focused on Oceania

children.heading: Definition of appropriate congressional committees
children.label: 1 Definition of appropriate congressional committees
children.text: In this subsection, the term “appropriate congressional committees” means the Committee on Foreign Relations of the Senate and the Committee on Foreign Affairs of the House of Representatives.

children.label: A
children.text: assign additional full-time equivalent personnel to the Office of Australia, New Zealand, and Pacific Island Affairs of the Bureau of East Asian and Pacific Affairs of the Department of State, including to the unit established under subparagraph (B), as the Secretary of State determines to be appropriate, in accordance with paragraph (4)(A); and

children.label: B
children.text: establish a unit in the Bureau of East Asian and Pacific Affairs of the Department of State to carry out the functions described in paragraph (3).

children.label: A
children.text: Managing the bilateral and regional relations with the Freely Associated States.

children.label: B
children.text: Supporting the Secretary of State in leading negotiations relating to the Compacts of Free Association with the Freely Associated States.

children.label: C
children.text: Coordinating, in consultation with the Department of the Interior, the Department of Defense, and other interagency partners as appropriate, implementation of the Compacts of Free Association with the Freely Associated States.

children.label: i
children.text: the use of existing flexible hiring authorities, including Domestic Employees Teleworking Overseas (DETOs); and

children.label: ii
children.text: the realignment of existing personnel, including from the United States Mission in Australia, as appropriate;

children.label: B
children.text: reduce the number of vacant foreign service positions in the Pacific Island region by establishing an incentive program within the Foreign Service for overseas positions related to the Pacific Island region; and

children.label: C
children.text: report to the appropriate congressional committees on progress toward objectives outlined in this subsection beginning 1 year from , and annually thereafter for 5 years.March 9, 2024
completion of projects and fulfillment of commitments or obligations;
termination of the Trust Territory Government and termination of the High Court;
health and education as a result of exceptional circumstances;
ex gratia contributions for the populations of Bikini, Enewetak, Rongelap, and Utrik; and
technical assistance and training in financial management, program administration, and maintenance of infrastructure.
